author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XXVI. 36-38.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then cometh Jesus with them unto a place called Gethsemane, and saith unto the disciples, Sit ye here, while I go and pray yonder.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He took with Him Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, and began to be sorrowful and very heavy: and He saith unto them, My soul is exceeding sorrowful, even unto death; tarry ye here, and watch with me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because they clung to Him inseparably, therefore He saith, “Tarry ye here, while I go away and pray.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it was usual with Him to pray apart from them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this He did teaching us in our prayers, to prepare silence for ourselves and great retirement.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He takes with Him the three, and saith unto them, “my soul is exceeding sorrowful, even unto death.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore doth He not take all with Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That they might not be cast down; but these He taketh that had been spectators of His glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: However, even these He dismisses: “And He went on a little farther, and prayeth, saying, Father, if it be possible, let this cup pass from me; nevertheless not as I will, but as Thou wilt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He cometh unto them, and findeth them sleeping, and saith unto Peter, What, could ye not watch with me one hour?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Watch and pray, that ye enter not into temptation; the spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not without reason doth He inveigh against Peter most, although the others also had slept; but to make him feel by this also, for the cause which I mentioned before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then because the others also said the same thing (for when Peter had said (these are the words), “Though I must die with Thee, I will not deny Thee; likewise also,” it is added, “said all the disciples”); He addresses Himself to all, convicting their weakness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they who are desiring to die with Him, were not then able so much as to sorrow with Him wakefully, but sleep overcame them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He prays with earnestness, in order that the thing might not seem to be acting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And sweats flow over him for the same cause again, even that the heretics might not say this, that He acts the agony.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore there is a sweat like drops of blood, and an angel appeared strengthening Him, and a thousand sure signs of fear, lest any one should affirm the words to be feigned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause also was this prayer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By saying then, “If it be possible, let it pass from me,” He showed His humanity; but by saying, “Nevertheless not as I will, but as Thou wilt,” He showed His virtue and self-command, teaching us even when nature pulls us back, to follow God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since it was not enough for the foolish to show His face only, He uses words also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again, words sufficed not alone, but deeds likewise were needed; these also He joins with the words, that even they who are in a high degree contentious may believe, that He both became man and died.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if, even when these things are so, this be still disbelieved by some, much more, if these had not been.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See by how many things He shows the reality of the incarnation: by what He speaks, by what He suffers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After that He cometh and saith to Peter, as it is said, “What, couldest thou not watch one hour with me?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: All were sleeping, and He re bukes Peter, hinting at him, in what He spake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the words, “with me,” are not employed without reason; it is as though He had said, Thou couldest not watch with me one hour, and wilt thou lay down thy life for me? and what follows also, intimates this self-same thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “Watch,” saith He, “and pray not to enter into temptation.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See how He is again instructing them not to be self-confident, but contrite in mind, and to be humble, and to refer all to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And at one time He addresses Himself to Peter, at another to all in common.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to him He saith, “Simon, Simon, Satan hath desired to have you, that he may sift you as wheat; but I have prayed for thee;” and to all in common, “Pray that ye enter not into temptation;” every way plucking up their self-will, and making them earnest-minded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, that He might not seem to make His language altogether condemnatory, He saith, “The spirit indeed is ready, but the flesh is weak.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For even although thou dost desire to despise death, yet thou wilt not be able, until God stretch forth His hand, for the carnal mind draws down.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again He prayed in the same way, saying, “Father, if this cannot pass from me except I drink it, Thy will be done,”showing here, that He fully harmonizes with God’s will, and that we must always follow this, and seek after it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And He came and found them asleep.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For besides that it was late at night, their eyes also were weighed down by their despondency.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the third time He went and spake the same thing, establishing the fact, that He was become man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the second and third time is in the Scriptures especially indicative of truth; like as Joseph also said to Pharaoh, “Did the dream appear to thee the second time?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For truth was this done, and that thou mightest be assured that this shall surely be.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He too once, and twice, and three times spake the same thing, for the sake of proving the incarnation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherefore came He the second time?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In order to reprove them, for that they were so drowned in despondency, as not to have any sense even of His presence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He did not however reprove them, but stood apart from them a little, showing their unspeakable weakness, that not even when they had been rebuked, were they able to endure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He doth not awake and rebuke them again, lest He should smite them that were already smitten, but He went away and prayed, and when He is come back again, He saith, “Sleep on now, and take your rest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet then there was need to be wakeful, but to show that they will not bear so much as the sight of the dangers, but will be put to flight and desert Him from their terror, and that He hath no need of their succor, and that He must by all means be delivered up, “Sleep on now,” He saith, “and take your rest; behold the hour is at hand, and the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He shows again that what is done belongs to a divine dispensation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He doth not this only, but also, by saying, “into the hands of sinners,” He cheers up their minds, showing it was the effect of their wickedness, not of His being liable to any charge.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Rise, let us be going; behold, he is at hand that doth betray me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For by all means He taught them, that the matter was not of necessity, nor of weakness, but of some secret dispensation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, as we see, He foreknew that Judas would come, and so far from flying, He even went to meet him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: At any rate, “While He yet spake, lo, Judas, one of the twelve, came, and with him a great multitude with swords and staves, from the chief priests and elders of the people.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seemly surely are the instruments of the priests! “with swords and staves” do they come against Him!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Judas, it is said, with them, one of the twelve.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again he calleth him “of the twelve,” and is not ashamed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now he that betrayed Him gave them a sign, saying, “Whomsoever I shall kiss, that same is He, hold Him fast.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Oh! what depravity had the traitor’s soul received.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For with what kind of eyes did he then look at his Master? with what mouth did he kiss Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Oh! accursed purpose; what did he devise?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What did he dare?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What sort of sign of betrayal did he give?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whomsoever I shall kiss, he saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He was emboldened by his Master’s gentleness, which more than all was sufficient to shame him, and to deprive him of all excuse for that he was betraying one so meek.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But wherefore doth He say this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because often when seized by them He had gone out through the midst, without their knowing it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nevertheless, then also this would have been done, if it had not been His own will that He should be taken.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It was at least with a view to teach them this, that He then blinded their eyes, and Himself asked, “Whom seek ye?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they knew Him not, though being with lanterns and torches, and having Judas with them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Afterwards, as they had said, “Jesus;” He saith, “I am He” whom ye seek: and here again, “Friend, wherefore art thou come?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For after having shown His own strength, then at once He yielded Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But John saith, that even to the very moment He continued to reprove him, saying, “Judas, betrayest thou the Son of Man with a kiss?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Art thou not ashamed even of the form of the betrayal? saith He.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nevertheless, forasmuch as not even this checked him, He submitted to be kissed, and gave Himself up willingly; and they laid their hands on Him, and seized Him that night on which they ate the passover, to such a degree did they boil with rage, and were mad.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: However, they would have had no strength, unless He had Himself suffered it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet this delivers not Judas from intolerable punishment, but even more exceedingly condemns him, for that though he had received such proof of His power, and lenity, and meekness, and gentleness, he became fiercer than any wild beast.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Knowing then these things, let us flee from covetousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that, that it was, which then drove him to madness; that exercises them who are taken thereby in the most extreme cruelty and inhumanity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, when it makes them to despair of their own salvation, much more doth it cause them to overlook that of the rest of mankind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And so tyrannical is the passing, as sometimes to prevail over the keenest lust.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore indeed I am exceedingly ashamed, that to spare their money, may indeed have bridled their unchastity, but for the fear of Christ they were not willing to live chastely and with gravity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore I say, let us flee from it; for I will not cease for ever saying this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For why, O man, dost thou gather gold?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why dost thou make thy bondage more bitter?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why thy watching more grievous?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why thy anxiety more painful?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Account for thine own the metals buried in the mines, those in the kings’ courts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed if thou hadst all that heap, thou wouldest keep it only, and wouldest not use it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if now thou hast not used the things thou possessest, but abstainest from them as though they belonged to others, much more would this be the case with thee, if thou hadst more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is the way of the covetous, the more they heap up around them, the more to be sparing of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But I know,” sayest thou, “that these things are mine.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The possession then is in supposition only, not in enjoyment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But I should be an object of fear to men, sayest thou.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, but thou wouldest by this become a more easy prey both to rich and poor, to robbers, and false accusers, and servants, and in general to all that are minded to plot against thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if thou art desirous to be an object of fear, cut off the occasions by which they are able to lay hold of thee and pain thee, whoever have set their hearts thereon.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hearest thou not the parable that saith, that the poor and naked man, not even a hundred men gathered together are ever able to strip?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he hath his poverty as his greatest protection, which not even the king shall ever be able to subdue and take.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The covetous man indeed all join in vexing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why do I say men, when moths and worms war against such a man?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why do I speak of moths?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Length of time is enough alone, even when no one troubles him, to do the greatest injury to such a man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is the pleasure of wealth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I see its discomforts, but do thou tell me the pleasure of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what are its discomforts? sayest thou: anxieties, plots, enmities, hatred, fear; to be ever thirsting and in pain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if any one were to embrace a damsel he loves, but were not able to satisfy his desire, he undergoes the utmost torment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Even so also doth the rich man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he hath plenty, and is with her, but cannot satisfy all his desire; but the same result takes place as some wise man mentions; “The lust of eunuch to deflower a virgin;” and, “Like an eunuch embracing a virgin and groaning;”so are all the rich.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why should one speak of the other things? how such a one is displeasing to all, to his servants, his laborers, his neighbors, to them that handle public affairs, to them that are injured, to them that are not injured, to his wife most of all, and to his children more than to any.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not as men does he bring them up, but more miserably than menials and purchased slaves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And countless occasions for anger, and vexation, and insult, and ridicule against himself, doth he bring about, being set forth as a common laughing stock to all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So the discomforts are these, and perhaps more than these; before one could never go through them all in discourse, but experience will be able to set them before us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But tell me the pleasure from hence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “I appear to be rich,” he saith, “and am reputed to be rich.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what kind of pleas ure to be so reputed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is a very great name for envy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I say a name, for wealth is a name only void of reality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Yet he that is rich,” saith he, “indulges and delights himself with this notion.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He delights himself in those things about which he ought to grieve.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “To grieve? wherefore?” asks he.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because this renders him useless for all purposes, and cowardly and unmanly both with regard to banishment and to death, for he holds this double, longing more for money than for light.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such a one not even Heaven delights, because it beareth not gold; nor the sun, forasmuch as it puts not forth golden beams.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But there are some, saith he, who do enjoy what they possess, living in luxury, in gluttony, in drunkenness, spending sumptuously.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: You are telling me of persons worse than the first.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the last above all are the men, who have no enjoyment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the first at least abstains from other evils, being bound to one love; but the others are worse than these, besides what we have said, bringing in upon themselves a crowd of cruel masters, and doing service every day to the belly, to lust, to drunkenness, to other kinds of intemperance, as to so many cruel tyrants, keeping harlots, preparing expensive feasts, purchasing parasites, flatterers, turning aside after unnatural lusts, involving their body and their soul in a thousand diseases springing therefrom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither is it on what they want they spend their goods, but on ruining the body, and on ruining also the soul therewith; and they do the same, as if any one, when adorning his person, were to think he was spending his money on his own wants.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that he alone enjoys pleasure and is master of his goods, who uses his wealth for a proper object; but these are slaves and captives, for they aggravate both the passions of the body and the diseases of the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What manner of enjoyment is this, where is siege and war, and a storm worse than all the raging of the sea?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if wealth find men fools, it renders them more foolish; if wanton, more wanton.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is the use of understanding, thou wilt say, to the poor man?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As might be expected thou art ignorant; for neither doth the blind man know what is the advantage of light.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Listen to Solomon, saying, “As far as light excelleth darkness, so doth wisdom excel folly.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But how shall we instruct him that is in darkness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the love of money is darkness, permitting nothing that is to appear as it is, but otherwise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For much as one in darkness, though he should see a golden vessel, though a precious stone, though purple garments, supposes them to be nothing, for he sees not their beauty; so also he that is in covetousness, knows not as he ought the beauty of those things that are worthy of our care.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Disperse then I pray thee the mist that arises from this passion, and then wilt thou see the nature of things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But nowhere do these things so plainly appear as in poverty, nowhere are those things so disproved which seem to be, and are not, as in self-denial.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But oh! foolish men; who do even curse the poor, and say that both houses and living are disgraced by poverty, confounding all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what is a disgrace to a house?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I pray thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It hath no couch of ivory, nor silver vessels, but all of earthenware and wood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, this is the greatest glory and distinction to a house.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to be indifferent about worldly things, often occasions all a man’s leisure to be spent in the care of his soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When therefore thou seest great care about outward things, then be ashamed at the great unseemliness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the houses of them that are rich most of all want seemliness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when thou seest tables covered with hangings, and couches inlaid with silver, much as in the theatre, much as in the display of the stage, what can be equal to this unseemliness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what kind of house is most like the stage, and the things on the stage?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The rich man’s or the poor man’s?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is it not quite plain that it is the rich man’s?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This therefore is full of unseemliness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What kind of house is most like Paul’s, or Abraham’s?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is quite evident that it is the poor man’s.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This therefore is most adorned, and to be approved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that thou mayest learn that this is, above all, a house’s adorning, enter into the house of Zacchæus, and learn, when Christ was on the point of entering therein, how Zacchæus adorned it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he did not run to his neighbors begging curtains, and seats, and chairs made of ivory, neither did he bring forth from his closets Laconian hangings; but he adorned it with an adorning suitable to Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What was this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “The half of my goods I will give,” he saith, “to the poor; and whomsoever I have robbed, I will restore fourfold.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: On this wise let us too adorn our houses, that Christ may enter in unto us also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These are the fair curtains, these are wrought in Heaven, they are woven there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Where these are, there is also the King of Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou adorn it in another way, thou art inviting the devil and his company.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He came also into the house of the publican Matthew.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then did this man also do?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He first adorned himself by his readiness, and by his leaving all, and following Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So also Cornelius adorned his house with prayers and alms; wherefore even unto this day it shines above the very palace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the vile state of a house is not in vessels lying in disorder, nor in an untidy bed, nor in walls covered with smoke, but in the wickedness of them that dwell therein.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Christ showeth it, for into such a house, if the inhabitant be virtuous, He is not ashamed to enter; but into that other, though it have a golden roof, He will never enter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that while this one is more gorgeous than the palace, receiving the Lord of all, that with its golden roof and columns is like filthy drains and sewers, for it contains the vessels of the devil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But these things we have spoken not of those who are rich for a useful purpose, but of the grasping, and the covetous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither is there amongst these, diligence nor care about the things needful, but about pampering the belly, and drunkenness, and other like unseemliness; but with the others about self-restraint.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore nowhere did Christ enter into a gorgeous house, but into that of the publican and chief publican, and fisherman, leaving the kings’ palaces, and them that are clothed with soft raiment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If then thou also desirest to invite Him, deck thy house with alms, with prayers, with supplications, with vigils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These are the decorations of Christ the King, but those of mammon, the enemy of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let no one be ashamed then of a humble house, if it hath this furniture; let no rich man pride himself on having a costly house, but let him rather hide his face, and seek after this other, forsaking that, that both here he may receive Christ, and there enjoy the eternal tabernacles, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might world without end.
